content.problemStatement:
Archi is a small boy from Uzhlyandia. For Chef's birthday, he prepared a tree with NNN vertices (numbered 111 through NNN). Each edge in this tree has a certain length.

There are MMM speedsters on the tree, numbered 111 through MMM. For each valid iii, speedster number iii starts at time tit_iti​ from vertex number uiu_iui​ and runs straight to vertex number viv_ivi​ with constant velocity sis_isi​. Before time tit_iti​, the speedster is not considered to be present in the tree.

Chef is interested in the following question: for each vertex, what is the smallest time when a speedster is present at this vertex?
constraints: the graph described on the input is a tree
the sum of NNN for all test cases does not exceed 2⋅1052 \cdot 10^52⋅105
the sum of MMM for all test cases does not exceed 2⋅1052 \cdot 10^52⋅105
the sum of NNN for all test cases does not exceed 5⋅1035 \cdot 10^35⋅103
the sum of MMM for all test cases does not exceed 5⋅1035 \cdot 10^35⋅103
**Subtask #2 (20 points):** the velocities of all speedsters are identical
**Subtask #3 (20 points):** the tree is a bamboo (a path)
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
Each of the next N−1N-1N−1 lines contains three space-separated integers uuu, vvv and www denoting an edge between vertices uuu and vvv with length www.
The next line contains a single integer MMM.
For each valid iii, the iii-th of the next MMM lines contains four space-separated integers uiu_iui​, viv_ivi​, tit_iti​ and sis_isi​ describing the iii-th speedster.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print NNN lines. For each valid iii, the iii-th of these lines should contain a single real number — the minimum time when the iii-th vertex is visited by a speedster, or −1-1−1 if it is never visited. Your answer will be considered correct if its absolute or relative error does not exceed 10−610^{-6}10−6.
